title: Actinocephalus denudatus
intro: Actinocephalus denudatus, commonly known as the "false buttonbush," is a plant species belonging to the family Eriocaulaceae. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in Brazil, to treat various health issues, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Actinocephalus denudatus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal activities. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to treat various health conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
taxonomy: Actinocephalus denudatus is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Eriocaulaceae. This plant is native to South America and belongs to the genus Actinocephalus. Further classification of Actinocephalus denudatus includes its order, Asparagales, and class, Liliopsida. The family Eriocaulaceae is a group of monocots commonly known as pipeworts. Actinocephalus denudatus is considered a member of the monocot family Eriocaulaceae.
